subject: Re: Importing Relion4 Refine3D star file to M
body:
Hi Israel,

You should be able to move forwards with
- particle positions
- shifts from refinement in pixels (rename rlnOriginXAngst to rlnOriginX and specify 1.00A/px for the shifts when importing. Same for Y)
- rlnMicrographName (not sure on the path details for SPA, in tomography it seems only the filename is important so this is likely the same?)

Good luck!

Alister

> On 22 Aug 2022, at 19:20, PirateFernandez <israel.s.fernandez@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hello!
> This is my first message here!
> I want to re-process a ribosome SPA dataset in Warp/M. Warp version 1.09 run quite well so I have all the frames aligned and ready to go. Now, starting M I can set up the populations (2 populations as I have to datasets) and hashes are generated I think properly. However, when I try to define the species corresponding to the Relion4 refine 3D job , I can import the half-maps, the mask but once I tried to import the run_data.star file, M crashes. Because this is a Relion4 star file, I though that perhaps I can remove the first loop of the start file and go back to old relion star format. With this modified star file M does work, it does not crashes and seems to be looking for the particles but, after a while it can't find any particle to map from this species to the populations.
> My specific question is, which relion star file format (in the sense of which columns and perhaps in which order) is M expecting the run_data.star to be? 
> Is having x/y coordinates,  micrograph name and the angles enough? 
> Additionally, do I need to specify the complete path to the micrograph name in my Windows machine in the star file? Perhaps that is the issue?
> Thanks!
> Israel
> 
> --
